title: pyrus aria subvar. angustifolia
intro: Pyrus aria subvar. angustifolia, also known as the Himalayan mountain apple, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. Its bark, leaves, and fruit have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Pyrus aria subvar. angustifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant include making tea from its leaves and bark, as well as using its fruit in jams and preserves, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of uses.
